info.title: Fitness API
info.description: REST API for the Fitness & Nutrition SPA. Call GET /sanctum/csrf-cookie then POST /api/auth/login before accessing protected endpoints.

200.description: Downloadable JSON with all user data

nutrient_reference_set.description: Metadata of the DRI reference set used (code/version/checksum/sources/limitations) — not the full reference value table.

nutrients.description: Canonical dynamic payload: any tracked nutrient code (all 17, not limited to the six legacy ones) mapped to a number or null. null = unknown/remove; 0 = a real declared zero. Unknown codes and legacy/nutrients value conflicts are rejected with 422.

Canonical dynamic payload: any tracked nutrient code (all 17, not limited to the six legacy ones) mapped to a number or null. null = unknown/remove; 0 = a real declared zero. Only codes explicitly present are touched. Unknown codes and legacy/nutrients value conflicts are rejected with 422.

errors.description: Map of CSV line number to error message.
Map of CSV line number to error message.

409.description: Instrument version outdated or consent required

200.description: Latest assessment with items, or null when none exists
Assessment and items deleted; historical goals keep their snapshots

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Local date (Y-m-d) in the user timezone

200.description: Goals with descriptive progress for the current local period
Daily hydration summary: plain water, dietary water and estimated total kept separate

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Local date (Y-m-d) in the user timezone

200.description: Plain-water entries for the local date

422.description: Invalid volume, future timestamp, daily limit, or idempotency conflict

200.description: Longitudinal hydration summary (max 365 days); no streaks, no forecast

422.description: Invalid range or range exceeding the maximum

201.description: Meal copied to target date (with warnings/differences)

portion_id.description: Omit for grams; set to log by portion count

quantity.description: Grams, or portion count when portion_id is set

201.description: Item added from food (grams or portion)
Item added from recipe (grams or servings)
Meal created from template (with warnings for missing sources)

200.description: Paginated list of the user's private foods

schema.description: Canonical shape uses `nutrition_basis` + `nutrients` (any tracked code). `input_mode` and the six legacy top-level nutrient fields are deprecated aliases still accepted; sending a legacy field and `nutrients` with different values for the same code returns 422.

serving_size_g.description: Required (or the legacy serving_* quartet) when nutrition_basis=per_serving.

energy_kcal.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.energy_kcal.

protein_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.protein_g.

carbohydrate_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.carbohydrate_g.

fat_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.fat_g.

fiber_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.fiber_g.

sodium_mg.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.sodium_mg.

schema.description: Partial update: omitted keys are left unchanged. Canonical shape uses `nutrition_basis` + `nutrients` (any tracked code, null clears the row to unknown, 0 persists a real zero). `input_mode` and the six legacy top-level nutrient fields are deprecated aliases still accepted.

energy_kcal.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.energy_kcal.

protein_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.protein_g.

carbohydrate_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.carbohydrate_g.

fat_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.fat_g.

fiber_g.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.fiber_g.

sodium_mg.description: Deprecated legacy top-level field; use nutrients.sodium_mg.

200.description: Tracked nutrient definitions in stable display order
Per-nutrient daily intake with status, coverage, reference and descriptive comparison
Detailed longitudinal report for one nutrient

422.description: Invalid period or range over the maximum

200.description: Longitudinal nutrient intake: logged-day averages, coverage, provenance, references and descriptive comparisons

422.description: Invalid period or range over the maximum

200.description: Reference set metadata, profile context and applicable references

parameters.name: include_nutrients
parameters.description: Sprint 4D: adds an optional nutrient_summary block (all 16 tracked nutrients)

post.summary: Save profile and calculate first nutrition target.

200.description: Paginated list of the user's recipes

post.summary: Handle an incoming new password request.
Send a password reset link to the given email.

Food.description: `is_favorite` is only present on endpoints that compute favorite state (catalog, search, recent items) — absent on my-foods CRUD responses. `portions` is only present on the food detail endpoint, not on list endpoints.

is_favorite.description: Absent on my-foods CRUD responses; present elsewhere.

portions.description: Only present on the food detail endpoint.

FdcPendingNutrient.description: A source nutrient that was not mapped to a tracked code: dimension/unit mismatch, or an explicitly prohibited conversion (e.g. IU to RAE/mcg, folate total/folic acid to DFE). Never silently converted.

mapped.description: Tracked nutrient code => amount for every FDC number that mapped cleanly.

is_locked.description: True for system-seeded canonical mappings; cannot be changed via the admin endpoint.

reason_code.description: Why the mapping is pending/ignored (e.g. a prohibited FDC number), when applicable.

Recipe.description: `instructions` and `ingredients` are only present on the recipe detail endpoint, not on list endpoints.

NutritionReportDay.description: `weight_kg` is only present when the report was requested with include_weight=true.

UnifiedSearchResponse.description: `message` is only present on the short-query (below minimum length) response.

max_value.description: Technical anti-abuse maximum, not a health recommendation.

Medas14Answers.description: All 14 answers are required. Booleans must be real JSON booleans; quantities must be real JSON numbers >= 0.

answer.description: Boolean for boolean items, number for quantity items.

DietQualityFocusCandidate.description: Optional, traceable focus suggestion. Alcohol and olive-oil quantity never appear here.

DietQualityGoalProgress.description: Descriptive progress for the current local period (ISO week Mon-Sun). `no_data` never counts as success.

DietQualitySummary.description: Descriptive summary only: no clinical bands, no risk language, no predictions.

StoreDietQualityGoalRequest.description: Title, threshold and scoring always come from the backend catalog; the client only picks a focus.

NutrientDefinition.description: Tracked nutrient metadata. Codes are the public contract; numeric IDs are never exposed.

NutrientCoverage.description: Item-level data coverage for one nutrient on one day. `coverage_pct` is null when items_total is 0.

NutrientQualityBreakdown.description: Counts of contributing items per data-provenance bucket. Never a universal quality score.

NutrientReferenceRange.description: Range mode never discloses which end corresponds to which sex; there is no midpoint field.

NutrientReference.description: One resolved population reference (RDA/AI/CDRR) for a nutrient. Exact mode carries `value`; range mode carries `minimum`/`maximum` and both are null in exact mode (and vice versa).

is_primary.description: Primary reference is used for comparison (e.g. sodium CDRR over AI).

NutrientReferenceContext.description: Resolved profile context (age/sex stratum) used to select references on a given local date. `age_years`/`sex_basis` are null when no profile context is available for that date.

NutrientAmount.description: Per-nutrient intake for one local day, with status, coverage, reference and a neutral descriptive comparison. `value` is the sum of KNOWN item amounts; null when nothing is known (never coerced to zero).

NutrientReferenceSegment.description: A run of consecutive local days sharing the same resolved reference context. Present only when the reference changed within the period; references are never averaged across segments.

PeriodNutrientIntake.description: Longitudinal per-nutrient summary. `average_value` is computed only over logged days with a fully known value ("Promedio de días registrados"); no-data days are excluded, never coerced to zero.

coverage.description: days_total, days_logged, days_with_known_value, days_with_partial_value, days_with_unknown_value, diary_coverage_pct, nutrient_data_coverage_pct.

NutrientDailyPoint.description: One point in a nutrient trend series. `value` is null on days with no known contribution — charts must not interpolate across a null point.

coverage.description: days_total, days_logged, days_with_known_value, days_with_partial_value, days_with_unknown_value, diary_coverage_pct, nutrient_data_coverage_pct.

DynamicNutrientInput.description: Canonical dynamic nutrient payload keyed by tracked nutrient code. Each value is a JSON number or null; omitted keys are left unchanged on update; null explicitly marks the nutrient unknown; 0 persists a real zero. Unknown keys, numeric strings, NaN/Infinity, negatives, arrays and objects are all rejected with 422.

FoodNutrientProvenance.description: Per-nutrient row on a food, including data origin and quality provenance (Sprint 4B/4C columns). `amount_per_100g` is only meaningful when the row exists; absence of a row for a tracked code means unknown, never zero.

data_origin.description: Null only for rows predating the Sprint 4B provenance columns.

source_reference.description: E.g. the FDC nutrient number when data_origin=external_import.

HydrationEntry.description: One manually registered plain-water entry. Represents ONLY plain water — never a meal log item, never a nutrient snapshot. local_date is server-derived from occurred_at in the user timezone.

HydrationCoverage.description: Coverage of the DIETARY water estimate only (diary items with a known water_ml value). Manual plain water is reported separately and is always "known" once stored.

HydrationReference.description: Population total-water AI (National Academies DRI for Water). Informational only — never a goal, never a percentage, never a "missing ml" prescription. Range mode never discloses a midpoint.

HydrationDailyResponse.description: plain_water_logged_ml, dietary_water_ml and estimated_total_water_ml are always reported separately and never fused. comparison is indeterminate unless status=complete.

average_label.example: Promedio de días con agua registrada

HydrationPeriodResponse.description: No streak, prediction, or "days compliant" fields — daily_points break the line with null on days without data (no forecast).

cookieAuth.description: Sanctum SPA cookie-based auth. Call /sanctum/csrf-cookie first, then POST /api/auth/login.
cookieAuth.name: fitness_session
